platforms.name: Perplexity

API Reference: New parameters documented — `input` (string or array), `models` (fallback chain), `preset`, `max_steps`. Tools renamed: WebSearchTool, FetchUrlTool, FunctionTool with expanded details. Streaming response types expanded. Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/api-reference
API Documentation: Added "Leveraging frontier AI models like GPT-5.4" as explicit capability. Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai

aidr.title: Vertex AI Agent Engine Code Execution GA
aidr.description: Promoted from Preview to GA — production agents can now execute arbitrary code with HIPAA compliance. Activity monitoring must track code execution invocations for injection attacks, unauthorized operations, and PHI exposure

aidr.title: Azure AI Foundry IQ — knowledge base agent connections
aidr.description: New Foundry IQ service connects agents to knowledge bases — creates data exfiltration vectors through knowledge base content accessible to agents. Data Lens must monitor Foundry IQ knowledge base queries and data access patterns

aidr.title: Azure AI Red Teaming Agent (preview)
aidr.description: Automated red teaming agent can probe AI systems for vulnerabilities — if misused or misconfigured, could expose security weaknesses. Activity monitoring must detect red teaming agent deployments and their target scope

aidr.title: OpenAI GPT-5 system prompt non-adherence
aidr.description: GPT-5 models reportedly ignoring system prompts — undermines guardrail enforcement and content policy controls. AIDR Findings must flag GPT-5 deployments where system prompt compliance is critical

aidr.title: OpenAI MCP server tools in ChatGPT developer mode
aidr.description: MCP tools now accessible in developer mode — expands the tool execution surface in ChatGPT, creating new vectors for unauthorized tool invocations

aidr.title: OpenAI Codex post-task freezing with token consumption
aidr.description: Codex freezing after task completion while consuming tokens — potential unmonitored agent activity after apparent task end. AIDR Findings should detect runaway agent sessions

aidr.title: Vertex AI Prompt Management SDK
aidr.description: Stored and versioned prompts via SDK — prompts are now persistent artifacts that could contain sensitive instructions or data. Data Lens should monitor prompt storage for sensitive content

aidr.title: Azure OpenAI API v1 GA versioning change
aidr.description: API version shifted from date-based (2024-10-21) to "v1" — Activity monitoring API integrations must update versioning or risk losing visibility

aidr.title: OpenAI GPT-Realtime-1.5 tool interaction bug
aidr.description: Text output mode broken when tools active — may cause silent failures in monitored agent workflows, reducing detection coverage

aidr.title: OpenAI custom MCP connectors not displaying all tools
aidr.description: Tools may be active but not visible to administrators — creates blind spots in Activity monitoring

aidr.title: OpenAI Sora-2 API video generation failures
aidr.description: API limitations on 15-20s videos may cause agents to retry or use workaround patterns that bypass monitoring

aidr.title: OpenAI GPT-5.4-mini billing discrepancy
aidr.description: 1M vs 10M token group billing may indicate usage metering issues affecting accurate activity volume tracking

aidr.title: Vertex AI Agent Engine pricing changes
aidr.description: Runtime charges for Sessions, Memory Bank, and Code Execution may cause organizations to reduce usage, impacting monitoring coverage

aidr.title: Windsurf improved MCP context management
aidr.description: Changes to MCP server context handling may affect detection of data flows through MCP tool invocations

aidr.title: OpenAI Atlas Browser interest
aidr.description: 105 replies signals OpenAI expanding into browser-based AI — future Activity monitoring scope may expand

aidr.title: Dia Chromium security feature description change
aidr.description: "Profile syncing to Google disabled" changed to "Settings upload after profile reset disabled" — verify actual security behavior

aispm.title: Vertex AI Claude 3 Haiku shutdown (August 23, 2026)
aispm.description: New deprecation timeline — Inventory must identify all Claude 3 Haiku deployments on Vertex AI and flag for migration. Violations should alert on continued usage approaching shutdown

aispm.title: Vertex AI Agent Engine CMEK support
aispm.description: Customer-managed encryption keys now available — AISPM Violations should flag Agent Engine deployments NOT using CMEK as a security posture gap

aispm.title: Vertex AI Explainable AI deprecated
aispm.description: Service deprecated March 16, 2026 — Inventory must flag any agents or workflows still relying on Explainable AI

aispm.title: Vertex AI AutoML Text deprecated
aispm.description: Migration to Gemini required — Inventory must map AutoML Text users and track migration status

aispm.title: Bedrock Claude Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6 availability
aispm.description: New model versions with full code examples — Inventory must map these deployments. Violations should check for deprecated model usage (Opus 4 EOL May 31, 2026)

aispm.title: Bedrock GPT-OSS-120B model
aispm.description: Significant model size increase (20B→120B) — Inventory must track organizations deploying this larger open-weight model

aispm.title: Vertex AI Claude Opus/Sonnet 4.6 partner models
aispm.description: Now available on Vertex AI — Inventory must discover cross-platform model deployments

aispm.title: Azure AI Foundry Local
aispm.description: Running LLMs on local devices — Inventory must track local model deployments that bypass cloud governance. Violations should flag ungoverned local AI deployments

aispm.title: Vertex AI Feature Store Optimized deprecated
aispm.description: Organizations must migrate — Violations should flag continued usage

aispm.title: Vertex AI Image generation preview endpoints retired
aispm.description: Preview endpoints no longer functional — Inventory should identify agents still using deprecated endpoints

aispm.title: Vertex AI Reasoning Engines API category
aispm.description: New API resource category — Inventory should map reasoning engine deployments as a distinct resource type

aispm.title: Vertex AI Confidential Computing GA for Workbench
aispm.description: Production-ready confidential VMs — Violations should flag Workbench instances NOT using Confidential Computing for sensitive data

aispm.title: Vertex AI Private Service Connect expanded
aispm.description: Broader private connectivity — Violations should flag agents using public endpoints when Private Service Connect is available

aispm.title: Vertex AI Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol
aispm.description: Multi-agent orchestration — Inventory must discover A2A topologies. Violations should check for unauthorized agent-to-agent communications

aispm.title: Azure AI Foundry Control Plane tracing
aispm.description: New tracing and monitoring capabilities — Inventory can leverage these for posture visibility

aispm.title: Windsurf enterprise feature expansion
aispm.description: Team conversation sharing, GitHub Reviews app, Google Docs knowledge base, Windows Group Policy — Inventory must track configurations. Violations should verify access controls

aispm.title: Perplexity API parameter expansion
aispm.description: New `input`, `models`, `preset`, `max_steps` and renamed tools — Inventory should update API surface tracking

aispm.title: Vertex AI DeepSeek-V3.2 and GLM models
aispm.description: New open-source model options — Inventory must map deployments with different security profiles

aispm.title: Power Apps version 3.26034 schedule change
aispm.description: First available pushed to Mar 23 — track version compliance

aispm.title: Vertex AI Virtual Try-On GA
aispm.description: New production model endpoint — Inventory should include virtual-try-on-001

aispm.title: OpenAI GPT-5.4-mini pricing discrepancies
aispm.description: Token pricing issues may affect cost governance

endpoint.title: Azure Foundry Local — LLMs on local devices
endpoint.description: Official Microsoft support for local LLM execution with HuggingFace compilation — new endpoint AI deployment vector bypassing cloud governance. Must include in endpoint inventory and policy enforcement

endpoint.title: Windsurf enterprise management expansion
endpoint.description: Windows Group Policy, team conversation sharing, Windsurf Reviews GitHub app — significant endpoint governance capabilities

endpoint.title: Windsurf Ubuntu system requirements raised
endpoint.description: glibc >= 2.31 and glibcxx >= 3.4.26 now required — endpoint inventory must verify developer machines meet new requirements. Older Ubuntu systems may lose Windsurf support

endpoint.title: Windsurf MCP context management improvements
endpoint.description: Changes to MCP server context handling affect data flows at the endpoint level

endpoint.title: Windsurf Knowledge base curation via Google Docs
endpoint.description: External knowledge source integration — potential data leakage vector through Google Docs connections

endpoint.title: Windsurf Netlify deployment integration
endpoint.description: Direct deployment from AI coding assistant may bypass security review processes

endpoint.title: Perplexity Electron desktop app
endpoint.description: New desktop client expands endpoint footprint — requires endpoint inventory tracking

endpoint.title: Perplexity tool renaming and expansion
endpoint.description: WebSearchTool, FetchUrlTool, FunctionTool — endpoint MCP integrations must update tool detection signatures

endpoint.title: OpenAI Codex CLI "speed feature"
endpoint.description: New execution mode being tested — may affect endpoint monitoring and token usage patterns

endpoint.title: OpenAI ChatGPT merchant integration
endpoint.description: Commerce features could enable financial transactions from AI endpoints

endpoint.title: OpenAI MCP server tools growing adoption
endpoint.description: 87 replies signals MCP tool ecosystem expansion at the endpoint level

endpoint.title: Cursor v2.6 formatting changes
endpoint.description: Section restructuring only — no new security-relevant capabilities

endpoint.title: Dia security feature description change
endpoint.description: Chromium disabled feature changed from "Profile syncing" to "Settings upload after profile reset" — verify no functional change

highlights: **Google Vertex AI** — Agent Engine Code Execution promoted to **GA** with **CMEK encryption** and **HIPAA compliance** support. **Claude 3 Haiku shutdown** announced for August 23, 2026. **Vertex Explainable AI deprecated** (March 16, 2026). **AutoML Text deprecated** — migrate to Gemini. **Virtual Try-On model GA**. Image generation preview endpoints retired March 19. Prompt management capabilities added to SDK. Claude Opus/Sonnet 4.6 and DeepSeek-V3.2 added as partner models. Agent Engine now charging for Sessions, Memory Bank, and Code Execution.

**Azure AI Foundry** — New **Foundry IQ** section (knowledge-base-connected agents), **AI Red Teaming Agent (preview)**, **Foundry Local** for running LLMs on local devices, Java SDK added. API versioning shifted to **"v1" GA** for Data Plane.

**Amazon Bedrock** — **Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6** listed with full code examples. **GPT-OSS-120B** model appearing (up from GPT-OSS-20B). Server-side tools and AgentCore Gateway integration documented.

**OpenAI Community** — **GPT-5 models not adhering to system prompts**. **GPT-Realtime-1.5 text output broken with tools active**. **MCP server tools in ChatGPT developer mode**. **Sora-2 API failing on 15-20s videos**. **/images/edits rejecting gpt-image models**. **Codex freezing post-task consuming excess tokens**. Atlas Browser feature interest growing. ChatGPT merchant integration questions surfacing.

**Windsurf** — Enterprise features expanded: team conversation sharing, Windsurf Reviews GitHub app, Google Docs knowledge base curation, Netlify deployment, Windows Group Policy. Ubuntu system requirements raised (glibc 2.28→2.31).
**Perplexity** — API updated: new `input`, `models`, `preset`, `max_steps` parameters. Tools renamed (WebSearchTool, FetchUrlTool, FunctionTool). Streaming response types expanded.

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot (M365)
details: Microsoft Graph API Changelog: Bicep infrastructure-as-code support added for User, Application, servicePrincipal, OAuth2PermissionGrant, appRoleAssignment, Group resources. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/

Lifecycle Workflows workflow creation limit raised from 50 to 100. New resources: attributeChangeTrigger, groupBasedSubjectSet, membershipChangeTrigger, insights. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/
Event Hubs change notifications will retire SAS authentication — migrate to Entra ID RBAC. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/
New agentSignIn resource with agentType enumeration for identity blueprint principals. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/
New selfServiceSignUp, signUpIdentity, signUpStatus resources for external ID tracking. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/
17 new protocol type members added (implicit flows, authorization code variants, client credentials, Kerberos, SSO, OBO). Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/

platforms.name: Dia Browser
details: v1.23.0 (Mar 19, Issue #17): GitHub Live Tab Groups — PR Status Celebrations animations, Automatic Cleanup on PR merge, Recently Completed PR Preview (hover to restore), enhanced navigation animations. Source: https://diabrowser.com/release-notes

v1.22.0 (Issue #16, Mar 11) expanded: Proactive Suggestions AI-curated, performance fixes (sluggishness/memory leaks), profile switching shortcuts (Ctrl+1,2,3), Command Bar routing to Chat and Google Search. Source: https://diabrowser.com/release-notes

Security page updated: Independent security firm assessment detailed (code reviews, architecture analysis, exploit testing). AI data retention policy clarified — never used for training. Prompt injection safeguard note: some attacks can cause unexpected style/tone shifts without compromising system security. Source: https://diabrowser.com/security

aidr.title: Microsoft Graph agentSignIn resource
aidr.description: New agentType enumeration enables tracking of agent identity principals — Zenity must update Activity monitoring to detect and classify agent-type sign-ins vs human sign-ins

aidr.title: Copilot Studio Work IQ tools (Preview)
aidr.description: Agents can now access real-time M365 files, emails, meetings, and chats — creates new data exfiltration vectors through agent-accessible work context. Data Lens must monitor Work IQ tool invocations

aidr.title: Amazon Bedrock Server-Side Tools
aidr.description: AgentCore Gateway tools execute server-side without client orchestration — Activity monitoring loses client-side visibility into tool execution. Must adapt detection to server-side tool call patterns

aidr.title: Anthropic Memory tool multi-platform expansion
aidr.description: Agents can now persist and recall information across sessions on Bedrock, Vertex AI, Azure AI — creates persistent data exposure vectors. AIDR Findings must detect sensitive data stored in agent memory

aidr.title: OpenAI MCP connector token refresh issue
aidr.description: MCP connector refreshing tokens on every tool call without session persistence — potential token leakage/replay attack surface

aidr.title: Microsoft Graph 17 new protocol types
aidr.description: Implicit flows, authorization code variants, client credentials, Kerberos, SSO, OBO — expand the attack surface for token-based attacks. Activity detection rules need updating

aidr.title: Microsoft Graph selfServiceSignUp resources
aidr.description: New signUpIdentity and signUpStatus resources for external ID tracking — potential for unauthorized external identity creation

aidr.title: Microsoft Graph SAS auth deprecation
aidr.description: Organizations must migrate Event Hubs change notification auth to Entra ID RBAC or lose notification delivery

aidr.title: Bedrock AgentCore Gateway enterprise integrations
aidr.description: Salesforce, Zoom, JIRA, Slack integrations — agents can now interact with sensitive enterprise systems. Activity monitoring needs to cover these tool invocations

aidr.title: Bedrock AgentCore Browser service
aidr.description: Playwright and BrowserUse integrations let agents browse autonomously — Data Lens must track websites and content accessed by browser-enabled agents

aidr.title: Vertex AI Agent Engine expansion
aidr.description: Code Execution, bidirectional streaming, and Memory Bank — agents gain persistent memory and code execution capabilities. Must monitor for data persistence and code injection risks

aidr.title: OpenAI platform log retention issue
aidr.description: Platform logs not deleted after 30 days as expected — data retention violation may affect compliance monitoring

aidr.title: Perplexity Agent API migration
aidr.description: /v1/agent endpoint with model behavior controls and research_loop_steps — new autonomous agent patterns to monitor

aidr.title: Microsoft Graph eDiscovery Beta APIs
aidr.description: Legal discovery workflows may expose sensitive data through new API surface

aidr.title: OpenAI ChatGPT pinned chats incident
aidr.description: Service disruption on Mar 21 — monitor for data accessibility issues

aidr.title: Anthropic structured outputs Vertex AI exclusion
aidr.description: Platform parity gap may affect detection consistency

highlights: **Microsoft Graph API** — Major batch of new API additions: Bicep IaC support, Lifecycle Workflows capacity doubled (50→100), eDiscovery Beta APIs, Schema Extensions GA in US Gov, agent identity tracking via agentSignIn resource, SAS authentication deprecation for Event Hubs change notifications, and 17 new protocol type members.

**Copilot Studio** — New March 2026 entry: Work IQ tools (Preview) connects Microsoft 365 Copilot agents to real-time M365 work context (files, emails, meetings, chats). Claude Opus 4.6 added as model option.

**Amazon Bedrock** — Six new open-weight models (DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, Kimi K2.5, Qwen3 Coder Next). Server-Side Tools Support in Responses API. OpenAI-Compatible Projects API on Mantle engine. Claude Opus 4 deprecation: migrate to Opus 4.1 before May 31, 2026.

**Bedrock AgentCore** — Now supports Google ADK and OpenAI Agents SDK frameworks. Gateway integrations expanded to Salesforce, Zoom, JIRA, Slack. Browser service supports Playwright and BrowserUse. Policy service supports Cedar policy language.

**Google Vertex AI** — Claude 3.5 Haiku deprecation (shutdown July 5, 2026). Gemini 3.1 Pro, 3.1 Flash Image, and Gemini 2.5 Flash with Live API Native Audio released. Agent Engine expansion with Code Execution, bidirectional streaming, and Memory Bank.

**Anthropic Claude API** — Models API now returns capabilities object for programmatic feature discovery. Files API and Skills API expanded with GET endpoints. Memory tool now available on Bedrock, Vertex AI, and Azure AI. Code execution free when combined with web search/fetch.
**Cursor v2.6** — Automatic stale code cleanup, dynamic instrumentation logging, enhanced MCP setup, clearer team/enterprise access controls, enhanced Slack integration.
**Windsurf** — GPT-5.4 Mini at 1x credits. New enterprise features: MDM config, Devin service key auth. Deprecated: Windsurf Browser, Knowledge Base, Legacy Mode. Apple M5 compatibility fix.

**OpenAI Platform Issues** — GPT-5.4-nano Priority Service Tier latency issues. MCP connector refreshing tokens on every tool call without session persistence. Codex severe performance degradation with multiple files.
**Dia Browser v1.23.0** — GitHub Live Tab Groups with PR Status Celebrations. Security page updated with independent assessment details and prompt injection safeguard acknowledgment.

highlights: **Cursor Composer 2 released (Mar 19)** — New frontier-level coding model with token-based pricing ($0.50–$7.50/M tokens). Major AI coding assistant update.
**Dia Browser v1.23.0 (Mar 19)** — New GitHub PR management features AND prompt injection safeguards now explicitly documented in security page.
**Windsurf v1.9577.42–43 (Mar 19)** — Two new builds with bug fixes; earlier builds added SKILL.md loading from `.windsurf/skills/`, cascade hooks, and model pinning.
**AWS Bedrock AgentCore API expanded** — 3 new API operations including `Evaluate` for programmatic agent evaluation, total now 40 operations.
**Azure AI Foundry docs updated (Mar 19)** — New "Create apps with code" section, Microsoft Agent Framework reference, agentic workflow evaluation capabilities.
**OpenAI Community** — gpt-realtime-1.5 is live; .NET Agents SDK libraries released; Codex credit consumption issues reported.
**Perplexity API Reference** — Tools section expanded with three distinct tool categories (Web Search, Fetch URL, Function Calls) and streaming SSE event types.
**Salesforce Spring '26** — Expanded release resources with new webinars, demo collections; Slack and Revenue Management now featured products.

highlights: **OpenAI SDK updates**: Python v2.29.0 and Node v6.32.0 add GPT-5.4 nano/mini model support and /v1/videos batch endpoint

**OpenAI stability concerns**: Multiple incidents on Mar 17-18 (ChatGPT 5.4 Pro errors, Sora failures, auth issues) -- all resolved; community reports Codex data loss vulnerability (agent file deletion outside project directories)
**Google Vertex AI**: Vertex Explainable AI deprecated (Mar 16); Vector Search 2.0 GA (Mar 5); partner model evaluations expanded
**Cursor rapid iteration**: 30+ marketplace plugins, Automations (always-on agents), JetBrains IDE support, Team Marketplaces with governance controls
**Windsurf**: GPT-5.4 Mini now available; Apple M5 hardware support
**Microsoft Copilot Studio**: Work IQ Preview for real-time M365 insights; Claude Sonnet 4.5 for Computer Use agents
**Anthropic**: $100M Claude Partner Network; Anthropic Institute launch; Mozilla Firefox security partnership
**Dia Browser**: v1.22.0 with AI-driven Proactive Suggestions

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot (M365)
details: M365 Admin Center latest updates through November 2025, with Copilot Search Admin experience (June 2025) and AI Administrator role (January 2025) as notable recent additions
Graph API Changelog shows active development in identity governance, lifecycle workflows, and audit capabilities as of February 2026
Copilot extensibility documentation covers declarative agents, custom engine agents, plugins, connectors, and the new Microsoft Agent 365 (early access preview)
https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/admin/whats-new-in-preview?view=o365-worldwide
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365-copilot/extensibility/

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot Studio
details: Extremely active development cadence with monthly feature releases
March 2026: Work IQ tools (Preview) for real-time work insights from M365
February 2026: Claude model integration (Sonnet 4.5 for Computer Use, Opus 4.6 for prompts), content moderation sensitivity controls
January 2026: Agent evaluations enhancements, Computer Use expansions with Cloud PC pooling, VS Code extension GA
November 2025: GPT-5 Chat GA, multi-agent orchestration, MCP server support, SharePoint metadata filters
Released versions page only shows August-September 2024 entries (may be incomplete)

platforms.name: Azure AI Foundry
details: Platform rebranded from "Azure AI Studio" to "Microsoft Foundry"
February 2026: GPT-Realtime-1.5 and GPT-Audio-1.5 models released
December 2025: GPT-image-1.5, improved transcription/TTS/realtime-mini models
October 2025: GPT-4o-transcribe-diarize, PII detection filter, Realtime API SIP support
August-September 2025: GPT-5 family (GPT-5, GPT-5-mini, GPT-5-nano, GPT-5-chat, GPT-5-codex), Sora video generation (text-to-video, image-to-video, video-to-video)
API Reference: GA version 2024-10-21, Control Plane 2025-06-01, with monthly previews

platforms.name: Salesforce AgentForce
details: Spring '26 release is live, themed around "Agentic Enterprise" — combining human expertise with AI productivity
AgentForce positioned as "the agent-driven layer of the Salesforce Platform" with Trust Layer architecture
Developer tools include: Agent API, Python SDK, Models API, Testing API, Mobile SDK (iOS/Android)
Agent Script provides hybrid natural language + programmatic expression authoring
Agentforce DX supports pro-code workflows via CLI and VS Code
Spring '26 spans Agentforce, Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Field Service, Marketing Cloud, Commerce, and Analytics

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: All three OpenAI URLs blocked automated access:
platform.openai.com/docs/changelog — 403
openai.com/index/ — 403
help.openai.com/en/articles/6825453-chatgpt-release-notes — 403
**Action needed:** Consider alternative data sources such as RSS feeds, OpenAI's API status page, or manual browser-based checks
https://platform.openai.com/docs/changelog

platforms.name: Claude Enterprise (Anthropic)
details: Documentation has migrated from docs.anthropic.com to platform.claude.com (301 redirects)
Latest models: Claude Opus 4.6 (Feb 5, 2026) and Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Feb 17, 2026)
API surface organized into: Model capabilities, Tools (server-side & client-side), Tool infrastructure, Context management, Files/assets
Key capabilities: 1M token context, adaptive thinking, batch processing (50% savings), citations, computer use, MCP connector, agent skills
Beta features: Files API, Skills API
Available on direct API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Azure AI

highlights: First run — baselines established for all monitored platforms
**All 3 OpenAI URLs returned HTTP 403** — these sources block automated fetching and need alternative access methods (browser scraping, RSS feeds, or API-based approaches)
**Anthropic docs URLs have migrated** from `docs.anthropic.com` to `platform.claude.com` — config should be updated
Microsoft ecosystem is very active: GPT-Realtime-1.5/GPT-Audio-1.5 just released in Feb 2026, Copilot Studio added Work IQ tools in Mar 2026
Salesforce Spring '26 release is now live with "Agentic Enterprise" as the primary theme
Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.6 (Feb 5) and Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Feb 17), raised $30B Series G at $380B valuation
Copilot Studio now supports Claude models (Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.5) for prompt building and Computer Use agents
